name: mosquito_gene_drive_island
premise: Public health minister Ana Tevasa is considering the release of gene-drive mosquitoes on a malaria-stricken island where childhood deaths have surged after insecticide resistance spread through local mosquito populations. Modeling from international researchers suggests the release could crash the disease-carrying population within two seasons and save hundreds of lives. Some island farmers, however, fear ecological side effects on pollination and birds, and they note that if the gene drive reaches neighboring islands, communities that never consented may be affected as well. International funders have offered to cover the full cost if the pilot begins this month, while local church leaders argue that transforming an entire species in the open environment crosses a moral line the government cannot later reverse. Ana must decide whether to authorize the release before the rainy season.

agents.name: Ana Tevasa
agents.identity: I am the public health minister for an island where policy is measured not in white papers but in children who either survive mosquito season or do not. I am drawn to bold tools, yet I know irreversible interventions deserve humility.
agents.goal: I want to reduce malaria deaths dramatically without imposing an uncontrollable ecological experiment on communities that may bear the consequences for generations. I need to decide whether collective health gains justify uncertain biological spillover.

agents.name: Father Micah Selo
agents.identity: I am a parish priest and community organizer whose congregants bury children lost to malaria every year. I also hear their fear that outsiders are asking us to become the proving ground for a technology that cannot be recalled once released.
agents.goal: I want Ana to pause the release until neighboring islands and local communities can give fuller consent because no government should act as though desperate need erases everyone's claim to a voice. I believe collective benefit must still respect moral boundaries and shared stewardship.

choices: I will authorize the gene-drive mosquito release before the rainy season because the projected reduction in malaria deaths is too significant to delay.
I will postpone the release until broader regional consent and ecological review are secured, even though that means another season of preventable malaria risk.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Ana Tevasa must decide whether to authorize the gene-drive mosquito release this month. What does Ana Tevasa do?
